Items.Title: 制作出色 CLI 应用程序的 14 个重要技巧 | 14 great tips to make amazing CLI applications
Items.Description:
我发现命令行工具是一种令人难以置信的方式来增加它们产生的代码的影响。

您是否编写了一个不错的库来包装 API 并将其连接到您的数据库模型？ 然后提供一个 CLI 工具，允许任何人查询 API、与 DB 模型交互、运行您提供的操作、发现所有可用功能并阅读 API 文档。 您现在可以轻松地编写脚本，在开发时执行您的代码的不同功能，创建预提交脚本，创建 CICD 脚本。

以下是我多年来收集的编写 CLI 工具的技巧。

使用命令行解析框架

虽然手动解析argv很有趣，但我直接使用全功能的命令行解析框架，例如 go 中的cobra 、 clap in rust、
在 python 中单击，在 PHP 中单击php-cli 。

这些不仅完善，它们通常允许您使用子命令，为多个 shell 生成自动完成脚本，处理 CLI 选项的漂亮打印并链接到配置框架（见下文）。

将工具作为单个文件分发，没有依赖关系

为确保您的 CLI 工具得到广泛采用，它必须易于安装。 没有人想安装 80 个节点模块或上帝保佑，设置一个 python virtualenv，只是为了使用你的工具。

  • 您还可以尝试在 Common Lisp 中转储世界图像。
  • 另一种选择是创建一个可以通过单个 docker 调用运行的 docker 映像，尽管这通常会使网络和文件 IO 更加复杂。
  • 一个折衷方案是通过你的语言包管理器（pip、npm、你有什么）使它成为一个易于安装的包

使文档成为工具本身的一部分

与其将文档作为单独的 wiki 分发，不如将其作为您工具的一部分。 不要吐出神秘的自动生成的参数列表，而是利用您的 CLI 框架和 TUI 库来提供漂亮的文档。

应该可以使用 CLI 工具来：

  • 查找有关动词和参数的所有信息（请参阅有关示例的下一点）
  • 获取有关工具本身及其操作原理的信息
  • 发现该工具提供的功能（请参阅有关构建语法的部分）

虽然我从未这样做过，但有些工具甚至提供了完整的教程作为其功能集的一部分！

考虑使用漂亮的库直接在命令行上呈现 markdown，例如glamor (golang) 或rich (python)。

将示例添加到您的文档中

帮助人们开始使用您的工具的最简单方法是提供大量示例。 这通常可以更直观地描述参数以及它们如何相互作用。 用它作为展示某些模式的机会，并提供一些关于为什么某个动词存在的背景信息。 以此为契机，展示使用 CLI 的复杂和高级方法，尤其是在您提供过滤和模板功能的情况下（见下文）。

让它漂亮

谁不喜欢漂亮多彩的 CLI 工具。 那里有很多终端 TUI 和样式管理库。 但是，不要忘记使您的工具与无色终端和 IO 管道兼容。

我在所有的charmbracelet库上玩得很开心，比如用于构建TUI 的bubbletea和用于造型的lipbgloss 。 使其具有交互性

对于某些应用程序（不是全部），我发现实现 CLI 工具的交互式版本很有用。 通常，我在控制硬件时会这样做，其中保持上下文很重要，并且在长时间运行的应用程序中更容易。

这可以像使用rlwrap包装一个简单的 stdin/stdout 循环一样简单，一直到使用功能齐全的 TUI 库，如bubbletea (golang)、 textual (python) 或imtui (c++)。

使用 asciinema 录制短教程

命令行应用程序的一大特点是记录终端会话非常轻量级，可以轻松转换为 gif 或呈现为网页。

像asciinema这样的工具非常适合展示现实生活中的工作流程。

精心制作 CLI 语法

这是我在构建 CLI 工具时一直发现的最困难的事情：设计一个好的语法。 在最好的情况下，动词和子命令是“直观的”：如果用户想要做某事，他们应该很容易猜出使用哪个命令。

这意味着如果您有一个get命令，则有一个set命令，或者对于每个可以操作的资源（例如files 、 hosts 、 drives ）都有一个适当的子命令。

一个令人困惑的语法是使用get --update来设置一个变量，或者使用动词ls-files来列出文件，使用hosts list子命令和动词来列出主机。

我发现我经常需要重写 CLI 动词结构几次，然后才能理解什么对我想要解决的问题有效。 不要试图在第一次就做好，事实上要避免第一次过于“框架化”或过于聪明。 在构建工具时随意添加您认为有用的动词和标志，然后在不介意从头开始重新启动动词和标志的地方进行第二次传递。 您可能会构建可重用的内部辅助函数。

添加结构化输入和输出

为了更轻松地传输和转换工具生成的数据，请考虑将结构化数据与人类可读数据一起输出。

我有生成的通用包装器：

  • 格式良好（和彩色）的表格化、人类可读的输出
  • 不同的预配置人类可读输出选项（例如--wide 、 --pretty 、 --oneline ）
  • 结构化 JSON 输出（这通常是最简单的，因为 CLI 操作的对象通常可以开箱即用地序列化）
  • 适用时，将数据作为 SQLite 数据库转储非常有用

类似地，不仅可以从 CLI，还可以从文件中轻松读取结构化输入。 这使得重新运行类似命令或在团队成员之间共享大批量数据变得更加容易。

有关这些概念的良好示例，请查看kubectl ，用于管理 kubernetes 资源。

添加过滤和聚合选项

结合前面添加结构化 IO 的概念，考虑添加过滤选项。 例如，您可以让用户选择仅将 3 个属性输出为表格数据，或按某个其他属性聚合计数。

作为实现您自己的过滤和聚合的替代方法，这并不总是那么容易，请考虑在您的 CLI 文档中使用jq 、 yq或xsv等工具添加过滤和处理数据的示例。

不要忘记提供大量示例（对现实世界越有用和适用越好），因为作为用户提出这些示例通常很耗时，尤其是当您只需要工具来完成某事时。 把你给出的每一个有用的例子想象成某人将来会体验到的一点快乐。

添加模板选项

如果您想进一步超越结构化输出，请考虑为用户提供提供输出模板的选项。 我总是发现为 CLI 编写模板非常耗时，但一旦我弄明白了它就非常有用。 所以不要吝啬例子！

轻松扩展您的 CLI

轻松添加新动词/扩展 CLI 工具。 这样做的一种常见模式是在使用未知动词XXX时查找名为yourtool-XXX的二进制文件是否存在，并将控制权传递给该二进制文件。 这就是gh （GitHub 客户端）和git的操作方式。

即使是简单的 shell 脚本动词也为用户提供了很好的扩展能力。 为了更大的影响，提供一个像gh这样的简单包装框架，它允许用 go 编写的扩展轻松调用主工具本身。

轻松配置 CLI

您可能希望在 CLI 中提供大量默认值和配置选项（凭据、URL 端点、输出设置）。

通过提供以下内容可以轻松配置这些：

  • 环境变量。 这具有使您的工具易于集成到 CICD 中的额外好处，并通过避免在命令行中列出凭据来提供安全性。 它还允许使用direnv 之类的工具轻松自定义每个目录或项目。
  • 配置文件。 一些命令行选项库提供了在命令行解析器旁边使用配置文件的选项（例如 golang 中的viper ）。 否则，使用 JSON、YAML 或 TOML 解析器读取默认值和覆盖没有什么比这更容易的了。
  • 提供动词来查询和更新您的配置文件。 许多现代 CLI 工具提供动词来与它们自己的配置进行交互。 很好的例子是git config ，它提供了一个丰富的动词来查询、删除、更新、设置 git 配置。 转储和查询当前信息（连同版本、平台、加载的环境和配置文件）可以轻松调试问题。
  • 提供全局和本地配置文件。 除了提供全局配置文件外，还允许用户提供本地配置文件。 使链接配置文件变得容易，这样您就可以拥有一个全局配置，然后是一个项目范围的配置（检入 git），然后是一个用户特定的覆盖配置（未检入 git）。

提供自动完成集成

对于用户来说，没有什么比按下 Tab 键并获得一个不错的自动完成更好的了。 如今，Shell 为自动完成提供了非常好的 UI，并提供了一个丰富的框架来利用这些 UI。

除了通过 CLI 参数解析框架使用内置的自动完成生成（见上文），考虑提供动态自动完成选项。 我过去在提供硬件控制工具时使用它来动态查询连接的设备。 自动完成将按 ID 显示已连接小部件的列表。

结论

它们通常很容易编写，对作者自己非常有用，可以作为在开发时练习代码的方法，也是“自我记录”和扩展应用程序库功能的好方法。

稍加思考和设计，CLI 就可以改变游戏规则。

那你呢？ 你会写 CLI 工具吗？ 如果是这样，您使用了哪些技巧？ 您喜欢 TUI 和交互性吗？



原文:

I find command-line tools an incredibly way to multiply the impact of the code they produce.

Did you write a nice library to wrap an API and connect it to your DB model? Then provide a CLI tool that allows anybody to query the API, interact with the DB model, run the operations that you provided, discover all the available functionality and read the API documentation. You can now easily write scripts that exercise different functionality of your code while developing, create pre-commit scripts, create CICD scripts.

Here are a collection of tips I gathered over the years writing CLI tools.

Use a command-line parsing framework

While it is fun to parse argv by hand, I go straight for full-featured command-line parsing frameworks like cobra in go, clap in rust,
click in python, php-cli in PHP.

Not only are these well established, they often allow you to use subcommands, generate autocompletion script for multiple shells, handle pretty-printing of CLI options and link up to configuration frameworks (see below).

Distribute the tool as a single file with no dependencies

To ensure widespread adoption of your CLI tool, it must be easily installable. No one wants to install 80 node modules or god forbid, setup a python virtualenv, just to use your tool.

  • Golang and Rust are perfect languages for this, as they allow you to easily compile static binaries for multiple platforms. You can also experiment with dumping world images in Common Lisp.
  • Another option is to create a docker image that can be run with a single docker call, although this often makes networking and file IO more complicated.
  • A compromise is to make it an easily installable package through your languages package manager (pip, npm, what have you)

Make documentation part of the tool itself

Instead of distributing the documentation as a separate wiki, make it part of your tool. Don't spit out cryptic auto-generated argument lists, but leverage your CLI framework and TUI library to provide beautiful documentation.

It should be possible to use the CLI tool to:

  • lookup all the information about verbs and arguments (see the next point about examples)
  • get information about the tool itself and its principles of operation
  • discover the functionality provided by the tool (see the section about a building a grammar)

While I have never done so, some tools even provide full tutorials as part of their feature set!

As complex the git command line can be, it provides great documentation entries for each verb.

Consider using a pretty library to render markdown straight on the command line, for example glamour (golang) or rich (python).

Add examples to your documentation

The easiest way to help people get started with your tool is to provide numerous examples. This often gives a more intuitive description of arguments and how they interact. Use it as an opportunity to show certain patterns and give some background information about why a certain verb exists. Use it as an opportunity to also show complex and advanced ways of using your CLI, especially if you provide filtering and templating functionality (see below).

git, aws, kubectl all provide examples that I have found extremely useful.

Make it pretty

Who doesn't like a pretty colorful CLI tool. There are a lot of terminal TUI and style management libraries out there. Don't forget to make your tool compatible to colorless terminals and IO pipes however.

I had a great time with all the charmbracelet libraries, like bubbletea for building TUIs and lipbgloss for styling. rich is a similar, awesome python library.

Make it interactive

For some applications (not all), I found implementing an interactive version of the CLI tool useful. Often, I have done so when controlling hardware, where keeping context is important, and much easier in a long running application.

This can be as easy as wrapping a simple stdin/stdout loop with rlwrap, all the way to using full featured TUI libraries like bubbletea (golang), textual (python) or imtui (c++).

Use asciinema to record short tutorials

A great feature of command line applications is that recording a terminal session is very lightweight, and can easily be transformed into a gif or rendered into a webpage.

Tools like asciinema are great to show real-life workflows.

Carefully craft a CLI grammar

This is what I consistently find the most difficult when building a CLI tool: designing a nice grammar. In the best of worlds, verbs and subcommands are "intuitive": if a user wants to do something, it should be easy for them to guess which command to use.

This means having a set command if you have a get command, or a proper subcommand for each resource that can be manipulated (for example files, hosts, drives ).

A confusing grammar would be using get --update to set a variable, or having a verb ls-files for listing files, and a hosts list subcommand and verb for listing hosts.

I find that I often have to rewrite the CLI verb structure a couple of times before I understand what works well for the problem I want to solve. Don't try to get it right the first time, in fact avoiding being too "frameworky" or clever the first time around. Liberally add the verbs and flags you find useful as you are building out your tool, and then do a second pass where you don't mind restarting the verbs and flags from scratch. Chances are that you build internal helper functions that can be reused.

Add structured input and output

CLIs are not just for humans, they are also extremely useful for machines. To make it easier to pipe and convert the data your tools generate, think about outputting structured data alongside human-readable data.

I have generic wrappers that generate:

  • tabulated, human-readable output that is nicely formatted (and colored)
  • different pre-configured human readable output options (for example --wide, --pretty, --oneline)
  • CSV and TSV formats for easy unix tool / spreadsheet consumption
  • structured JSON output (this is usually the easiest, as the objects manipulated by the CLI are often serializable out of the box)
  • when applicable, dumping data out as a SQLite database is extremely useful

Similarly, make it easy to read structured input, not just from the CLI, but also from files. This makes it much easier to rerun similar commands or share big batches of data amongst team members.

For good example of these concepts, look at kubectl , used to managed kubernetes resources.

Add filtering and aggregation options

In conjunction with the previous concept of adding structured IO, consider adding filtering options. For example, you could give a user the option to only output 3 attributes as tabular data, or to aggregate counts by a certain other attribute.

As an alternative to implementing your own filtering and aggregating, which is not always easy, consider adding examples where you filter and process data by using tools like jq, yq or xsv in your CLI documentation.

Don't forget to give copious examples (the more useful and applicable to the real-world, the better), as these are often time-consuming to come up as a user, especially when you just need the tool to get something done. Think of each useful example you give as a little nugget of pleasure that someone will experience in the future.

Add templating options

If you want to go even a step further beyond structured output, consider giving the user the option to provide output templates. Tools like kubectl or aws allow you to pass your own go-template inspired templates, making it possible to create exactly the output needed.

kubectl get no -o go-template='{{range .items}}{{if .spec.unschedulable}}{{.metadata.name}} {{.spec.externalID}}{{"\n"}}{{end}}{{end}}'

I always found writing templates for CLIs quite time-consuming, but extremely useful once I figured it out. So don't skimp on examples!

Make it easy to extend your CLI

Make it easy to add new verbs / extend the CLI tool. A common pattern to do so is to lookup if a binary called yourtool-XXX exists when an unknown verb XXX is used, and pass control to that binary. This is how gh (GitHub client) and git operate.

Even simple shell script verbs provide great extension capability for users. For even bigger impact, provide a simple wrapper framework like gh does, that allows extensions written in go to easily call the main tool itself.

Make it easy to configure your CLI

Chances are that you will want to provide a lot of default values and configuration options in your CLI (credentials, URL endpoints, output settings).

Make it easy to configure these by providing:

  • environment variables. This has the added benefit of making your tool easy to integrate in CICD, and provide security by avoiding listing credentials in your command line. It also allows for easy customization per directory or project, using a tool like direnv.
  • configuration files. A few command line option libraries provide the option to use a configuration file alongside the command line parser (for example viper in golang). Otherwise, nothing is easier to use a JSON, YAML, or TOML parser to read in defaults and overrides.
  • provide verbs to query and update your config file. A lot of modern CLI tools provide verbs to interact with their own configuration. Good examples are git config, which provides a rich verb to query, remove, update, set the git configuration. Dumping and querying the current information (alongside version, platform, loaded environments and config files) makes it easy to debug issues.
  • provide global and local configuration files. Besides providing a global configuration file, allow users to provide local configuration files. Make it easy to chain configuration files, so that you can have a global config, followed by a project-wide config (checked into git), followed by a user specific override config (not checked into git).

Provide autocompletion integration

Nothing is nicer for the user than just pressing tab and getting a nice autocompletion. Shells these days provide really nice UIs for autocompletion, and a rich framework to leverage these.

Besides using the builtin autocompletion generation by CLI argument parsing frameworks (see above), think about providing dynamic autocompletion options. I have used this in the past to dynamically query connected devices when providing a hardware control tool. Autocompletion would show a list of connected widgets by ID.

Conclusion

CLIs and TUIs are incredibly effective tool for the developer. They are usually easy to write, extremely useful for the author themselves as ways to exercise their code while developing, and a great way to "self-document" and extend the functionality of the application our library.

With a bit of thought and styling, CLIs can be turned into game changers.

What about you? Do you write CLI tools? If so, what tricks have you been using? Are you into TUIs and interactivity?
Items.Author: wesen

Items.Title: 在 Remix 中渲染降价 | Rendering markdown in Remix
Items.Description:
普通 Markdown 和 MDX 格式。

本文将向您展示为您的 remix 项目使用 markdown 是多么容易。

在 Remix 中使用纯 markdown 文件

让我们为我们的项目创建一个新目录，以便开始使用 Remix 中的纯 Markdown 文件。
我们将此文件夹称为blog 。 在此文件夹中，创建一个my-first-article.md文件并将以下内容放入其中。

 --- meta : title : " My first article" description : " Fully powered by Markdown" --- # Hello World! I'm amazing markdown content ## How cool!

现在只需运行您的网站并访问/blog/my-first-article端点！

您将受到完全呈现的降价的欢迎！

你可能想知道为什么看起来都一样？
我们的源代码包含所有的标题元素，但由于我们添加了零样式，我们看到没有它的所有内容。

我将在后续文章中告诉你更多关于添加排版插件的信息。

在 Remix 中渲染 MDX

除了普通的 Markdown，我们还可以选择 MDX，比如超级酷、稍微动态的 Markdown 版本。

首先在博客目录中创建一个mdx-sample.mdx文件。

 --- componentData : name : Chris --- import Name from "~/components/name"; # Hello MDX! <Name {... attributes.componentData } />

如您所见，我们在顶部设置了一些组件数据，然后导入了一个组件。

该组件然后呈现该组件数据。

让我们快速设置这个超级基础组件。
在你的app文件夹中创建一个components目录。

创建name.tsx文件并将以下组件粘贴到其中。

 const Name = ({ name }: { name : string }) => < div > Hello { name } < /div> ; export default Name ;

一个超级简单的组件，它呈现一个声明hello {name}的 div。

该名称将从降价文件中接收。

让我们看看当我们打开这个文件时它的样子。

您还可以在GitHub 上找到完整的代码示例。

感谢您的阅读，让我们联系吧！

感谢您阅读我的博客。 随时订阅我的电子邮件通讯并在Facebook或Twitter 上联系



原文:

Another cool out-of-the-box feature of Remix is that it supports markdown.

Both plain markdown and MDX format.

This article will show you how easy it is to get started with markdown for your remix project.

Using plain markdown files in Remix

Let's create a new directory for our project to get started with plain markdown files in Remix.
We'll call this folder blog. (Since we want to keep our posts for now)

Inside this folder, create a my-first-article.md file and place the following contents inside of it.

---
meta: title: "My first article" description: "Fully powered by Markdown"
--- # Hello World! I'm amazing markdown content ## How cool!

Now simply run your website and visit the /blog/my-first-article endpoint!

You'll be welcomed by the markdown fully rendered!

You might wonder why it all looks the same?
Our source code has all the heading elements, but since we added zero styling, we see everything without it.

I'll tell you more about adding the typography plugin in a follow-up article.

Rendering MDX in Remix

Besides plain markdown we can also opt for MDX, like super cool, slightly more dynamic version of markdown.

Start by creating an mdx-sample.mdx file in the blog directory.

---
componentData: name: Chris
--- import Name from "~/components/name"; # Hello MDX! <Name {...attributes.componentData} />

As you can see, we set some component data at the top section and then import a component.

This component then renders this component data.

Let's quickly set up this super basic component.
Create a components directory inside your app folder.

Create the name.tsx file and paste the following component into it.

const Name = ({ name }: { name: string }) => <div>Hello {name}</div>;
export default Name;

A super simple component that renders a div that states hello {name}.

The name will be received from the markdown file.

Let's look at how it looks when we open this file.

You can also find the complete code samples on GitHub.

Thank you for reading, and let's connect!

Thank you for reading my blog. Feel free to subscribe to my email newsletter and connect on Facebook or Twitter

Items.Title: 在 React 中渲染后设置焦点在输入字段上的方法？ | Ways to Set Focus on an Input Field After Rendering in React?
Items.Description:
某些用户在他们的网络会话中大部分时间都用双手使用键盘。 他们浏览网页、搜索、关闭标签等。无论他们做什么，他们都不喜欢将手移到鼠标上，而只是舒适地使用搁置的手动键盘。

集中这些用户，我们可以通过直接关注登录页面的电子邮件字段或评论表单的反馈字段等输入字段来帮助他们。

表中的内容

  1. 设置 React 项目

  2. 使用 React Hooks useRef 自动对焦

  3. 使用 React 类组件自动对焦

  4. 结论

输出结果：

我们在 HTML 中完成的自动对焦也可以在 ReactJS 中完成，但在进一步之前，我们需要创建一个 React 项目并查看项目结构以与您必须在项目中实现的内容/位置相关联以使用自动对焦。

要创建项目，请打开命令提示符或 PowerShell 到要在其中创建项目的文件夹。

第一步：创建一个名为 auto-focus-test 的项目

npx create-react-app auto-focus-test

第 2 步：转到创建的项目文件夹 auto-focus-test

cd auto-focus-test

文件夹结构将如下所示：

第 3 步：现在 src 文件夹中有一个 App.js 文件。 会有一个默认的 React 应用代码； 只需用下面给出的代码覆盖该代码，即可创建一个以电子邮件为标签的简单文本输入字段。

 import React from 'react'; export default function App() { return ( <div className="App"> <label>Email: </label> <input id="name" /> </div> ); }

为了在组件渲染时聚焦它，我们必须使用 React Hook useEffect，它在特定状态变量更改时被调用，或者我们可以提供一个空数组以确保它在组件渲染时只渲染一次。

 import React, { useEffect } from 'react'; export default function App() { useEffect(() => { // Instruction we give here will render once component gets rendered }, []); return ( <div className="App"> <label>Email: </label> <input id="name" /> </div> ); }

现在，要指向一个特定的 JSX 元素，我们需要使用 React 的 useRef Hook 给它一个引用。 这里是 ⁢input type=”text” />，渲染该页面/组件后将重点关注的方面。

 import { useRef, useEffect } from 'react'; export default function App() { const inputRef = useRef(); useEffect(() => { // Instruction we give here will render once component gets rendered }, []); return ( <div className="App"> <label htmlFor="name">Name: </label> <input id="name" ref={inputRef} /> </div> ); }

在这里，inputRef 是一个变量，它将存储 ⁢input type=”text” /> 元素的引用。 要访问它，我们需要使用像 inputRef.current 这样的 current 关键字，并且由于我们必须告诉 React 专注于此，我们可以在 useEffect Hook 中使用 focus() 方法。

 import { useRef, useEffect } from 'react'; export default function App() { const inputRef = useRef(); useEffect(() => { inputRef.current.focus(); }, []); return ( <div className="App"> <label htmlFor="name">Name: </label> <input id="name" ref={inputRef} /> </div> ); }

每次刷新页面或在新选项卡中打开它时，您都会注意到输入字段将始终聚焦。 在使用类组件的解释的另一种方法之后，下面给出了相同的输出参考。

首先，我们需要让我们的 App.js 文件成为一个类组件。 为此，只需将以下代码粘贴到 App.js 中并保存即可。

 import React, { Component } from "react"; class App extends Component { render() { return ( <div className="App"> <label>Email: </label> <input id="name" /> </div> ); } } export default App;

由于我们在功能组件中使用了 useEffect，因此我们需要使用 componentDidMount 作为类组件的 useEffect 的替代品。 这将在组件呈现时调用一次。

 import React, { Component } from "react"; class App extends Component { componentDidMount() { this.refInput.focus(); } render() { return ( <div className="App"> <label>Email: </label> <input id="name" /> </div> ); } } export default App;

现在我们将引用标签来告诉 React 专注于那个特定的组件。

 import React, { Component } from "react"; class App extends Component { componentDidMount() { this.refInput.focus(); } render() { return ( <div className="App"> <label>Email: </label> <input id="name" ref={(input) => { this.refInput = input; }} /> </div> ); } } export default App;

现在每次使用任何组件进行渲染时，无论是类还是函数式 App.js，它都会如下所示，我们可以直接在输入字段中输入一个值：

在这里，我们完成了在使用 Class 和 Functional 组件每次渲染该组件之后对输入字段的关注。 如果您需要解决 React 中的技术问题，请聘请 Bosc Tech 的 React 专家来采用 React 开发环境。 我们基本上都遵循了相同的方法。 我们通过 useEffect 和 componentDidMount 决定何时聚焦，然后我们给出参考并调用 focus()。

通过这种方式，我们可以为用户提供流畅的互联网冲浪，而无需让他们将手从键盘移到鼠标，反之亦然。 这种微小的外观变化或改进可能会在冲浪时影响主要用户。

感谢您的阅读。 希望你喜欢阅读。 请与我们分享您的想法。 所以，我们可以改进我们的内容。



原文:

There are certain users who use the keyboard with both of their hands for most of their time on their web sessions. They travel webpages, search, close tabs, etc. Whatever they do, they prefer not to shift their hand on the mouse and just comfortably work with resting hands-on keyboard only.

Centralizing these users, we can help them by directly focusing on input fields like the login page’s Email field or the review form’s feedback field.

Table of content

  1. Autofocus in HTML

  2. Setting up React project

  3. Autofocus using React Hooks useRef

  4. Autofocus using React class components

  5. Conclusion

1. Autofocus in HTML:

The autofocus attribute of the HTML tag is a boolean attribute.

The default value of this is false, but if we mention this, it tells the browser to focus on that particular input field, and the user can directly start entering values.

<html>
<body> 
<h1>The autofocus attribute</h1> 
<form>
     <label for="username">Username:</label>
     <input type="text" id="uname" name="uname" autofocus> //Will be focused
     <br><br>
     <label for="email">Email:</label>
     <input type="text" id="email" name="email"> //Won't be focused
</form>
</body>
</html>

Output Result:

2. Setting up react project:

The autofocus we did in HTML can also be done in ReactJS, but before going further, we need to create a React project and look into the project structure to relate with what/where you have to implement in your project to use autofocus.

To create a project, open Command Prompt or PowerShell to the folder where you want to create a project.

Step 1: Create a project of name auto-focus-test

npx create-react-app auto-focus-test

Step 2: Go to created project folder auto-focus-test

cd auto-focus-test

The folder structure will look like:

Step 3: Now there is an App.js file inside the src folder. There will be a default React app code; just overwrite that code with the below-given code to create a simple text input field with Email as the label.

import React from 'react';

export default function App() {
  return (
    <div className="App">
      <label>Email: </label>
      <input id="name" />
    </div>
  );
}

3. Autofocus using React hooks

To focus it when the component renders, we have to use React Hook useEffect, which gets called when particular state variable changes, or we can give an empty array to make sure it renders only once when the component renders.

import React, { useEffect } from 'react';

export default function App() {
  useEffect(() => {
    // Instruction we give here will render once component gets rendered
}, []);

  return (
    <div className="App">
      <label>Email: </label>
      <input id="name" />
    </div>
  );
}

Now, to point to a particular JSX element, we need to give it a reference with the useRef Hook of React. Here it is ⁢input type=”text” />, an aspect that will be focused on after rendering that page/component.

import { useRef, useEffect } from 'react';

export default function App() {
  const inputRef = useRef();

  useEffect(() => {
    // Instruction we give here will render once component gets rendered
  }, []);

  return (
    <div className="App">
      <label htmlFor="name">Name: </label>
      <input id="name" ref={inputRef} />
    </div>
  );
}

Here, the inputRef is the variable that will store the reference for the ⁢input type=”text” /> element. To access this, we need to use a current keyword like inputRef.current, and as we have to tell React to focus on this, we can just use focus() method inside the useEffect Hook.

import { useRef, useEffect } from 'react';

export default function App() {
  const inputRef = useRef();

  useEffect(() => {
    inputRef.current.focus();
  }, []);

  return (
    <div className="App">
      <label htmlFor="name">Name: </label>
      <input id="name" ref={inputRef} />
    </div>
  );
}

Every time you refresh the page or open it in a new tab, you will notice that the input field will always be focused. The output reference for the same is given below after another method using the class component’s explanation.

4. Autofocus using class component

First, we need to make our App.js file a class component. To do so, just paste the below code inside App.js and save it.

import React, { Component } from "react";

class App extends Component {
  render() {
    return (
      <div className="App">
        <label>Email: </label>
        <input id="name" />
      </div>
    );
  }
}

export default App;

As we used useEffect in the Functional component, we need to use componentDidMount as an alternative to useEffect for the class component. This will be called once when the component renders.

import React, { Component } from "react";

class App extends Component {
  componentDidMount() {
    this.refInput.focus();
  }

  render() {
    return (
      <div className="App">
        <label>Email: </label>
        <input id="name" />
      </div>
    );
  }
}

export default App;

Now we will give reference to the tag to tell React to get focus on that particular component.

import React, { Component } from "react";

class App extends Component {
  componentDidMount() {
    this.refInput.focus();
  }

  render() {
    return (
      <div className="App">
        <label>Email: </label>
        <input id="name" 
          ref={(input) => { this.refInput = input; }}
        />
      </div>
    );
  }
}

export default App;

Now every time you render with any of the components, whether class or functional App.js, it will look precisely like the below, and directly we can enter a value in the input field:

5. Conclusion

Here we accomplished focusing on the input field after each render of that component with both the Class and Functional components. If you need a solution for your technical problems in React, hire React experts from Bosc Tech to adopt React development environment. We basically followed the same method in both. We decided on when to focus by useEffect and componentDidMount, and then we gave reference and called focus().

By this, we can give the user smooth surfing on the internet without making them shift their hands from keyboard to mouse and vice versa. This tiny looking change or betterment can impact primary users while surfing.

Thank you for reading. Hope you enjoyed the reading. Please share your thoughts with us. So, we can improve our content.
Items.Author: kuldeeptarapara

Items.Title: Angular 13 检测屏幕的宽度和高度教程 | Angular 13 Detect Width and Height of Screen Tutorial
Items.Description:
更新 TypeScript 模板
从'@angular/core'包中导入HostListener API，定义变量get screen width和getScreenHeight，使用HostListener绑定windowresize事件来获取window resize时的屏幕尺寸和宽度。

更新 src/app/app.component.ts 文件中的代码。

 import { Component, HostListener } from '@angular/core'; @Component({ selector: 'app-root', templateUrl: './app.component.html', styleUrls: ['./app.component.scss'] }) export class AppComponent { public getScreenWidth: any; public getScreenHeight: any; ngOnInit() { this.getScreenWidth = window.innerWidth; this.getScreenHeight = window.innerHeight; } @HostListener('window:resize', ['$event']) onWindowResize() { this.getScreenWidth = window.innerWidth; this.getScreenHeight = window.innerHeight; } }

更新 HTML 模板
在此步骤中，您必须打开 Angular HTML 模板文件并使用双花括号定义变量以在浏览器上打印屏幕或窗口大小。

请更新 src/app/app.component.html 文件中的代码。

 <div class="container text-center mt-5"> <p>Window width: <strong>{{ getScreenWidth }}</strong></p> <p>Window height: <strong>{{ getScreenHeight }}</strong></p> </div>

参考：
https://www.positronx.io/angular-detect-width-and-height-of-screen-tutorial/ https://www.positronx.io/angular-detect-width-and-height-of-screen-tutorial/



原文:

Update TypeScript Template
Import HostListener API from ‘@angular/core’ package, define variables get screen width and getScreenHeight, use the HostListener to bind window to resize event to get the screen size and width on window resize.

Update the code in src/app/app.component.ts file.

import { Component, HostListener } from '@angular/core';

@Component({
  selector: 'app-root',
  templateUrl: './app.component.html',
  styleUrls: ['./app.component.scss']
})
export class AppComponent {

  public getScreenWidth: any;
  public getScreenHeight: any;

  ngOnInit() {
      this.getScreenWidth = window.innerWidth;
      this.getScreenHeight = window.innerHeight;
  }

  @HostListener('window:resize', ['$event'])
  onWindowResize() {
    this.getScreenWidth = window.innerWidth;
    this.getScreenHeight = window.innerHeight;
  }

}

Update HTML Template
In this step, you have to open the angular HTML template file and define the variables using the double curly braces to print the screen or window size on the browser.

Please update the code in src/app/app.component.html file.

<div class="container text-center mt-5">
  <p>Window width: <strong>{{ getScreenWidth }}</strong></p>
  <p>Window height: <strong>{{ getScreenHeight }}</strong></p>
</div>

Reference:
https://www.positronx.io/angular-detect-width-and-height-of-screen-tutorial/
Items.Author: dhanush9952

Items.Title: 作为开发人员如何找到下一份远程工作 | How to find your next remote job as a developer
Items.Description:
开发人员不想再在公司办公室工作了！ 这是最容易找到远程工作的工作之一，所以这里有一些提示可以帮助您！

领英

如果您还没有个人资料，只需创建一个并填写您所有的编程技能。 然后......等待招聘人员，我每天收到大约 5/10 份工作机会，但我从来没有在上面发帖！

工作委员会

有许多远程职位的工作委员会。 公司付费列出他们的工作，他们正在等待申请人！
这是一个清单：

聚会

个人网站

如果个人网站在 Google 上被很好地索引，它可以帮助您找到工作！ 对于前端开发人员来说，这可能是一个很好的策略，您可以直接在您的网站上展示您的技能！ 不要忘记指向您的 github 的链接；）



原文:

Developers don't want to work at the company office anymore ! And it's one of the jobs where it's the easiest to find a remote job, so here's some tips in order to help you !

Linkedin

Linkedin is full of bullshitter but it's also a great place to find a job as a remote developer. If you don't have a profile yet, just create one and fill it with all your programming skills. And then... just wait for recruiter, I receive like 5/10 job offers per day and I never post on it !

Job boards

There are many job boards for remote positions. Companies pay to list their jobs and they are waiting for applicants !
Here's a list :

Meetup

Meetup a platform to find events, and many are for developers, there are bound to be some around you and you can meet people whose companies are hiring!

Personal website

A personal website can help you to find a job if it's well indexed on Google ! It can be a good strategy for front-end developers, you can show your skills directly on your website ! Don't forget a link to your github ;)
Items.Author: pvhk

Items.Title: 为什么您的 :hover 选择器不起作用 | Why Your :hover Selector isn't Working
Items.Description:
这篇文章适合任何有 CSS 基础知识的人

您是否曾经申请过尝试使用 CSS hover选择器，但它似乎对您不起作用？ 或者更糟糕的是它适用于错误的元素？！？

如果你像我一样，那么它已经发生了很多。

很长一段时间我都被难住了。

也就是说，直到现在。

它在细节中

让我们看一个悬停效果的例子：

 .parent1 :hover { background-color : red ; }

你猜这个类会在悬停时将 parent1 类的背景颜色更改为红色，对吧？

那你就错了。

现在看看这个：

 .parent1 :hover { background-color : red ; }

您是否相信这会将 parent1 类的背景颜色更改为 red ？

如果是这样，那你就对了。

但是有什么区别呢？ 如果你不能区分，你会被原谅 - 我知道我一开始没有。

它是:hover之前的空格字符。

对于这样一个看似无害的空间来说，这是一个相当大的差异。

比较

看看下面的例子，看看我的意思。


这个故事的主旨 ？

注意间隔 ！

我希望这已经消除了关于为什么悬停效果对您不起作用的任何困惑！

欢迎在下方提问、评论或贡献！

如果你觉得很慷慨，你可以通过下面的链接给我买杯咖啡（是的，这都是为了咖啡，我在写作的时候喝了很多 ☕）



原文:

The post is suitable for anyone with a basic knowledge of CSS

Have you ever applied tried to use the CSS hover selector but it just doesn't seem to work for you ? Or worse yet it applies to the wrong elements ?!?

If you're like me then it has happened a lot.

And for a long time I was stumped.

That is, until now.

Its in the Details

Lets take a look at an example of a hover effect:

.parent1 :hover{ background-color:red;
}

You'd guess that class would change the background colour of the parent1 class to red on hover right ?

Well you'd be wrong.

Now take a look at this one :

.parent1:hover{ background-color:red;
}

Would you believe that this one would change the background colour of the parent1 class to red ?

If so, you'd be correct.

But what is the difference ? You'd be forgiven if you can't tell the difference - I know I didn't at first.

It's the space character before the :hover.

The space character in CSS is a descendant combinator ,which in this case means it will apply the hover effect to all the child elements of the element - not the element itself.

That's a pretty big difference for such a seemingly innocuous space.

Comparison

Take a look at the below examples to see what I mean.


The Moral of the Story ?

Mind The Gap !

Slán go fóill

I hope this has cleared up any confusion about why your hover effects weren't working for you !

Feel free to ask questions, comment or contribute below!

And if you're feeling generous you can buy me a coffee with the link below ( and yes its all for coffee, I drink a copious amount of it while writing ☕ )

Items.Title: 我的谷歌华沙（波兰）SDE 大学毕业生角色经历 | My Google Warsaw(Poland)SDE university Grad Role Experience
Items.Description:
大家好👋

我有机会在校外面试谷歌华沙（波兰）的软件工程师职位（大学毕业生）。 故事很戏剧化🙈。 希望你喜欢它。

选择状态：❌

面试经历：✔️

招聘经验：✅(OK)

我是怎么申请的？

大约在 2 月底，我收到了来自 Google 招聘人员的这封邮件，邮件说我的简历已入围 2022 年 Google SDE 大学毕业生职位，我被要求提供面试的首选日期。
收到邮件后，我在 2 天内回复了招聘人员关于我的信息。

采访

与招聘人员的电话是第一次面试。 我参加了电话，她告诉我我已入围面试轮🥳（这个电话发生在我回复招聘人员的邮件之后）。 我的招聘人员在面试时问了什么，我什么也不说，只说我过去的经历，我在哪里实习以及如何实习。 这很容易，在此之后，我被列入了下一次技术面试的候选名单，她让我知道会有什么轮次和轮次。 我问我可以花大约1个月的时间来准备，答案很简单，这取决于角色开放的时间，我很幸运我有1个月的时间来准备。 然后在4月的第一周，我的面试被安排了，我在中间做了任何可能的准备，现在面试来了，面试官很好，但他说话不那么直接，跳到问题，然后他解释了问题。 思考了 5 分钟后，我给了他蛮力解决方案。 然后我们讨论了解决方案的时间复杂度，他告诉我优化我的解决方案，我花了很多时间优化它（负面点）然后他说我写我的蛮力解决方案，我解释了他，采访结束了最后一个问题是关于我问他在谷歌的经历。

一周之内，我收到了招聘人员的一封邮件，说我们不会因为需要优化的解决方案而继续前进。



原文:

Hi everyone 👋

I got the opportunity to interview with Google Warsaw(Poland) for Software Engineer Role(University Grad), off campus. The story is quite dramatic 🙈. Hope you enjoy it.

Selection Status : ❌

Interview Experience: ✔️

Recruitment Experience: ✅(OK)

How I applied?

It was on around of end of February when I received this mail from a Google Recruiter, which said that my resume had been shortlisted for the Google SDE university Grad role 2022, and I was asked for preferred dates for the Interview.
As soon as I received the mail, I replied to the Recruiter within 2 days about me.

Interviews

The phone call with the recruiter was 1st interview. I attended the phone call, wherein she informed me that I was shortlisted for the Interview Round 🥳 (this call happened after my mail response to the recruiter). What my recruiter asked during an interview, I will say nothing, just my past experience, where and how I interned. It was easy, after this, I was shortlisted for the next technical interviews, she made me aware of what and how many rounds will be there. I asked can I take around 1 month time to prepare, and the answer was simple it depends on much time the role is opened, I was lucky I got 1 month time to prepare. then in the 1st week of April, my interview was scheduled, and I did whatever was possible to prepare in-between times, now interview came and the interviewer was good but he speaks less directly and jumped to questions, then he explained the question. I gave the brute force solution to him after thinking for 5 mins. then we discussed the time complexity of the solution, he told me to optimize my solution, I have taken much time in optimizing it (negative point) then he said me to write my brute force solution, I explained him, and the interview ended with the last question about I asked him his experience at Google.

It, within a week I got a mail from the recruiter that we are not moving forward just because an optimized solution is needed.
Items.Author: pawanupadhyay10

Items.Title: JS 中的 Promise 及其功能 | Promises in JS and its functions
Items.Description:
在学习 JavaScript 时，每个人都会遇到“Promises”这个话题，作为初学者可能很难理解 Promise
我会尽我最大的努力让你明白承诺，就在这段旅程中陪伴我直到最后。

他们有 3 个状态：

  1. 待办的
  2. 被拒绝
  3. 已解决又名已完成

现在，让我们了解这三种状态：

  1. 待处理：当您不知道某个操作是否会完成时，这是 Promise 的初始状态🤔。

  2. 已解决：确认执行操作时处于挂起状态😎。

承诺链
代码示例：

fetch(myRequest) .then((response) => { if (!response.ok) {

抛出新错误（

状态：${ response.status }`);
} }

}) .then((response) => { myImage.src = URL.createObjectURL(response); }) .then((response) => { myImage.src = URL.createObjectURL(response);

});` });`



原文:

While learning JavaScript everyone comes across this topic of "Promises", as a beginner it can be difficult to understand promises
I will try my best to make you understand about promises, just stay with me on this journey till the end.

Promises in JS are just like promises in real life, for example if some one has promised you something that they will perform some action upon your request then there is some probability that they will perform that and there is some probability that they will not perform that action.

Promises in JS are also similar. They have 3 states:

  1. Pending
  2. Rejected
  3. Resolved a.k.a Fulfilled

Now, let's understand about these 3 states:

  1. Pending: It is initial state of the promise when you don't know whether an action will be done or not 🤔.

  2. Rejected: It is state after pending when it confirmed that the action is not performed or cannot be performed 🙁.

  3. Resolved: It is state after pending when it confirmed that the action is performed 😎.

Promises are performed asynchronously but JS is a synchronous language, if didn't know about it before I would suggest you to read about this (Read about: Call stack, Web APIs, event loop, microtask queue).

Promise Chaining
Promise chaining is a way of executing two or more aysnc tasks which are related to previous output received

Code Example:

fetch(myRequest) .then((response) => { if (!response.ok) {

throw new Error(

HTTP error! Status: ${ response.status }`);
}

}) .then((response) => { myImage.src = URL.createObjectURL(response);

});`

Items.Title: CSS Flex-box 快速指南 | CSS Flex-box Quick Guide
Items.Description:
如果您是前端开发人员并且主要在您网站的样式部分面临一些困难时期，那么本文适合您🤗。

网站的 flexbox 主要用于布局和对齐容器的子项。

基本

上图是 CSS 中 flex 属性如何工作的基本构建块。

  • 主尺寸：总尺寸，即主容器的“宽度/高度”，以主轴为准。
  • 交叉尺寸：总尺寸，即主容器的“宽度/高度”，以沿交叉轴为准。
  • 主轴：它是沿该轴布置项目的行/列，如图中它在一行中。 这会根据 flex-direction 属性而改变。
  • 交叉轴：垂直于主轴的轴。

特性

  • 所以首先这里有一些 flex 的容器属性。

展示

 .container { display: flex; }

上图中显示的绿色背景是主 flex 容器，它被赋予了 flex 的 display 属性。 它会直接放下它的孩子。

弹性方向

如第一个示例本身所示

.container { // possible values flex-direction: row/column/row-reverse/column-reverse; }



弹性包装

默认值为“no-wrap”，表示项目倾向于将自身放入单个行或列中。

 .container { // possible values flex-wrap: no-wrap / wrap / wrap-reverse }

从图像中可以看出，由于“无包装”的默认属性，项目被挤压并且倾向于对齐并适合自己进入单行。

因此，通过将flex-wrap属性的值设置为wrap ，允许项目为它们提供尽可能多的可用空间，并且由于允许换行，它将转到下一行，它将从上到下换行到多行。

通过将其设置为wrap-reverse ，它的行为类似于 wrap 但方向相反，即从下到上。

证明内容

此属性设置沿主轴对齐。

 .container { // possible values justify-content: flex-start / flex-end / space-around / space- between / center / space-evenly.

  • 对齐项目

    它设置了沿横轴的对齐方式。


     .container { // possible values align-items: center / flex-start / flex-end / baseline / stretch }

    所以这些是需要在父级上使用的属性。 您可以通过更改上面列出的属性的值来玩弄给定的沙箱并学习，因为边做边学是最佳实践。

    下次找你！！！



原文:

If you are a Frontend Developer & facing some hard times mainly with the styling part of your website this article is for you🤗.

The flexbox for a website is mainly used for purpose of layout, and alignment for the children of a container.

Basics

The figure shown above is the basic building block of how the flex property works in CSS.

  • Flex Container: It is the main container to which CSS display: flex property will be given.
  • Flex Items: Children of the main container which will laid out according to the flex-direction property.
  • Main Size: Total size i.e "width/height" of the main container in whichever is along the main axis.
  • Cross Size: Total size i.e "width/height" of the main container in whichever is along the cross axis.
  • Main Axis: It is the axis along which items are laid out either row/column as in the figure it is in a row. This changes according to the flex-direction property.
  • Cross Axis: It is the axis perpendicular to the main axis.

Properties

  • Properties for flex are divided mainly into two different parts i.e the property for "container" & property for "items".
  • So first here are few container properties for flex.

display

.container {
   display: flex;
}

The green background shown in the above image is the main flex container to which the display property of flex is given. It will laid down its children directly.

flex-direction

The default property for flex-direction is row. As shown in first example itself

.container {
  // possible values
  flex-direction: row/column/row-reverse/column-reverse;
}



The flex-direction property will set the main-axis & items are aligned according to the value of direction.

flex-wrap

The default value is "no-wrap" means items will tend to fit itself into a single row or column.

.container {
  // possible values
  flex-wrap: no-wrap / wrap / wrap-reverse
}

As it's understood from the image the items are squeezed & tend to align & fit themselves into a single row because of default property of "no-wrap".

So by setting flex-wrap property with its value as wrap the items are allowed to fit as much available space to them & it will go to next line since wrapping is allowed it will wrap to multiple lines from top to bottom.

By setting it as wrap-reverse it behaves similar to wrap but in opposite direction i.e bottom to top.

justify-content

This property set alignment along main-axis.

.container {
  // possible values
  justify-content: flex-start / flex-end / space-around / space- 
  between / center / space-evenly.

  • flex-start: it is default value items are laid towards starting of flex-direction.
  • flex-end: items are laid towards end of flex-direction.
  • center: items are placed at the center.
  • space-around: items laid down & have equal spaced around them.
  • space-between: there is only space between the items of container & not between the borders of container & the items.
  • space-evenly: items are equally spaced around the edges & between themselves also.

align-items

It sets out alignment along the cross-axis.


.container {
  // possible values
  align-items: center / flex-start / flex-end / baseline / stretch
}

So these are the properties which needs to be used on parent for the flex. You can play around with the given sandbox & learn by changing the values for the above listed properties because learning while making is best practice.

https://codesandbox.io/s/nervous-bassi-ckrh4x?file=/src/styles.css

PS: Feedback's are welcome!! Catch you next time !!!

Items.Title: 响应式网页设计中要避免的主要错误 | Top Mistakes To Avoid During Responsive Web Design
Items.Description:
但是，开发和维护网站的响应式网页设计可能是一项累人的工作。 当您的网站流量低于预期水平时，您可能会感到恼火。 特别是在投入大量金钱和时间之后。

也许很多用户在他们选择的设备上无法正确查看您的网站并停止访问。

那么，如何确保您的网站在各种移动设备中都能响应并正确呈现？ 让我们来看看一些必须避免的错误，这些错误肯定会降低您网站的受欢迎程度和排名。

你知道HTML Prettify — 将 HTML 字符串/文件格式化为你喜欢的缩进级别。

使用大型媒体元素

这是一个非常常见的问题，发生在包含高分辨率图像和视频内容的网站中。 在网络覆盖较差的区域或物理内存较少的设备中，这将导致页面加载非常缓慢。 研究表明，普通互联网用户在网站上花费的时间不到一分钟，他们会在其中决定是留下来进一步探索还是离开网站。 如果您的网站加载时间超过一分钟，用户将离开该网站并转到另一个提供相同功能的网站。 这将导致您的业务损失并降低在搜索引擎中的排名。

使固定 -

  • 将 4k 和 2k 图像保留在桌面上，并使用低分辨率图像在使用移动设备打开网站时显示。

  • 删除对您的网站没有任何价值的不必要的视频。

  • 使用矢量图像而不是 .png，因为它们是可缩放的并且尺寸非常小。

复杂的导航和索引

开发人员往往会忘记，在响应式网页设计中，他们应该从用户的角度思考。 由于大屏幕，带有大量菜单和子项的集群导航栏在桌面上浏览起来很舒服。 但在小屏幕中，当用户不得不滚动浏览导航窗口中的菜单项数量时，他们会感到困惑和沮丧。

使固定 -

  • 对菜单和子项目进行分类并以某种方式排列它们，以便用户在返回上一个菜单或弄清楚哪个按钮执行什么操作时不会遇到任何问题。

  • 如果页面很长，请保留一个“滚动到顶部”按钮，这样可以节省滚动到页面顶部的工作量。

  • 为移动设备保留一个固定的标题，这样用户就不必在每次想要移动到另一个页面或另一个菜单项时向上滚动。

嘿，您知道Ember 测试吗？它是一个插件，可帮助您编写对 DOM 更改更具弹性的测试。

按钮和手势不起作用

许多设计师未能在设计网页的同时牢记触摸和手势功能。 经常观察到，按住、滑动、点击和放大效果不适用于响应式测试。 由于字体较小，用户经常无法点击按钮。

使固定 -

  • 请记住，由于鼠标指针很小，因此单击桌面上的项目更容易。 在移动设备中，用户必须使用他们的拇指或食指，这几乎是一毫米厚。 调整按钮的字体和大小，以便最终用户在导航时不会遇到任何问题。

  • 每个项目之间至少保持 20 像素，以避免接口导致的错误。

  • 应该在真实设备上手动完成单元测试，而不是在模拟器上进行测试，以确保最终用户可以舒适地使用应用程序。

隐藏不必要的内容

网站通常包含非常复杂的 UI 元素。 这可能包括多级表单、数据表、计算器、高级搜索表单等，这可能会产生问题，因为在 6 英寸的小手机屏幕上，用户需要处理的信息太多。 事实是，如果您的目标是移动设备，并且您拥有一个包含太多信息的复杂网站，那么就不可能适应您网站的全部内容。

使固定 -

  • 在设计阶段彻底规划网站，并决定在不同设备中显示哪些内容以及隐藏哪些内容。

  • 优化网站的移动视图，去除不必要的元素，只关注主要内容。

  • 使用下拉菜单或可折叠列等元素，让用户可以根据需要访问隐藏的功能。 向移动友好的测试人员寻求帮助。

还可以学习Express 测试— 并在线测试您的 Express 网页的响应能力和移动设备兼容性。

考虑屏幕尺寸

通常，编写媒体查询时会考虑到设备。 例如，有针对 iPhone、Samsung Galaxy、iPad 等设备的特定媒体查询。这在早期的移动设备数量有限时就可以使用。 但随着几乎每天都有新设备推出，这将产生问题。 观察到网站在 iPhone 上运行良好，但在屏幕尺寸略大于它的其他设备上却不行。

使固定 -

  • 对于响应式断点，请考虑不同的屏幕尺寸，例如超大、大、中、小和微型屏幕。

  • 不要在媒体查询中写入固定屏幕宽度，而是写入最小和最大设备宽度。

  • 继续测试您的网站并观察市场。 每当启动新的移动设备时，使用LambdaTest等基于云的测试平台，并检查您的网站是否在其中正确呈现。 执行屏幕分辨率测试。

在通过您的网站向全世界宣传您的想法时，您网站的响应能力应该是您的首要任务。 仔细设计和测试您的网站，牢记上述因素，并确保世界各地的用户可以使用他们选择的设备正确浏览您的网站。



原文:

The relevance of RWD(Responsive Web Design) is not only limited to content or marketing strategy, the number of devices on which your website gets properly rendered matters a lot if you wish your website to rank higher in SERPs(Search Engine Result Pages). RWD is also a key factor for increasing the end user engagement for any website. However, developing and maintaining the responsive web design of a website can prove to be a tiring job. You may get irritated when your website traffic is below the expected level. Especially, after investing a considerable amount of money as well as time.

Maybe a lot of users couldn’t view your website properly in their choice of device and stopped visiting.

So, how to ensure that your website is responsive and is rendered properly in all kinds of mobile devices? Let’s take a look at some must-avoid mistakes that will definitely bring down your site’s popularity and ranking.

Do you know about HTML Prettify — Formats a HTML string/file to your preferred indentation level.

Using Large Sized Media Elements

This is a very common issue that occurs in websites containing high-resolution images and video content. In an area with poor network coverage or a device with less physical memory, this will lead to very slow page loading. Studies show that an average internet user spends less than a minute in a site, within which they decide whether to stay and explore further or leave the website. If your website is taking more than a minute to load, the user will leave the website and move to another that offers the same features. This will lead to loss of your business and reduced ranking in the search engine.

Fix –

  • Leave the 4k and 2k images for desktop and use low-resolution images to be displayed when the site is opened using a mobile device.

  • Remove unnecessary videos that do not add any value to your website.

  • Use vector images instead of .png as they are scalable and have very less size.

Ensure Responsive Web Design Of Your Website On 50+ Mobile Views With LT Browser

Complicated Navigation and Indexing

Often developers tend to forget that in responsive web design, they should think from the perspective of a user. A clustered navigation bar with lots of menus and subitems is comfortable to explore in the desktop because of the large screen. But in small screen, the user gets confused and frustrated when they have to scroll through the number of menu items in the navigation window.

Fix –

  • Categorize the menu and sub items and arrange them in a way so that the user does not face any problem in going back to the previous menu or figuring out which button does what.

  • Keep a “scroll to top” button in case of a lengthy page that will save the effort of scrolling up to the top of the page.

  • Keep a fixed header for the mobile device so that user does not have to scroll up every time they want to move to another page or another menu item.

Hey did you know about Ember Testing — It is an addon that helps you write tests that are more resilient to DOM changes.

Buttons and Gestures Not Working

Many designers fail to design web pages while keeping the touch and gesture feature in mind. It is often observed that press and hold, swiping, tap and zoom in effects do not work for responsive test. Users often fail to click on buttons because of small font size.

Fix –

  • Remember, clicking an item on a desktop is easier because of the small size of the mouse pointer. In mobile, users have to use their thumb or index, which is almost a millimetre thick for this purpose. Adjust the fonts and size of buttons so that end users don’t face any problem while navigating.

  • Keep a minimum of 20 pixels between each item to avoid errors caused by interfacing.

  • Instead of testing on an emulator, unit testing should be done manually on real devices to ensure that the application can be used comfortably by an end user.

Hiding Unnecessary Content

Websites often contain UI elements that are quite complicated. This may include multi-level forms, data table, calculator, advanced search forms, etc. which may create a problem because, in a small 6-inch mobile screen, it is too much information to be processed by a user. The truth is, if you are targeting a mobile device and you have a complicated website with too much information, it is impossible to fit the entire content of your site.

Fix –

  • Plan the website thoroughly during the designing phase and decide what content to show and what to hide in different devices.

  • Optimize the mobile view of website and remove unnecessary elements and focus only on the primary content.

  • Use elements like dropdowns or collapsible columns that will allow the user to access the hidden functionality if they want to. Take help from a mobile friendly tester.

Also learn Express Testing — and Test your Express webpages for responsiveness and mobile device compatibility online.

Keeping Screen Size into Account

Often, media queries are written keeping the device into account. For example, there are device specific media queries for iPhone, Samsung Galaxy, iPad, etc. This used to work earlier when there was a limited number of mobile devices. But with new devices being launched almost daily, this will create a problem. Websites are observed to work perfectly in iPhone but not in another device with a screen size slightly larger than it.

Fix –

  • For responsive breakpoints, consider the different screen sizes like extra-large, large, mid, small and micro screens.

  • Instead of writing fixed screen width in the media query, write minimum and maximum device width.

  • Keep on testing your website and observe the market. Use cloud-based testing platforms like LambdaTest whenever a new mobile device is launched and check if your website is rendered properly in it. Perfrom screen resolution tests.

Responsiveness of your website should be your top priority while populating your idea with the world through your website. Design and test your website carefully keeping in mind the factors stated above and ensure that your website can be explored properly by users all around the world using the device of their choice.

Items.Title: 停止在节点后端使用 require() | STOP using require() in node backend
Items.Description:
对于所有节点开发人员，您需要停止在新项目中使用 require()。 该节点已经支持模块，本教程将告诉您它们之间的区别以及使用什么来代替 require() 并且还将深入了解模块。

你在用什么

const express = require ( ' express ' ) // common js

你应该使用什么

import express from ' express ' // es module

你有没有看到 ？？ 第二个代码看起来有多好。 第一个是 commonjs 语法，它从源头出现在 node 中，用于导入库，第二个首先在浏览器中引入，然后来到 node.js。

它使代码具有如此多的可读性、现代性和非冗长性。

如何使用它 ？

这简单。

  1. 初始化新节点项目。
  2. 转到您的 package.json。

  3. 添加以下内容。

     "type" : "module" ,

  4. 默认情况下，当您初始化项目时，其设置为 commonjs。

  5. 就是这样，现在开始使用现代 javascript。

常见模式

我将向您展示以模块格式实现的 commonjs 代码，而不是解释它，以便您可以立即开始它，并且将来当您对如何以模块格式执行某些操作感到困惑时，也可以回到本文。

输入

// cjs const express = require ( ' express ' ) // mjs import express from ' express '
 // cjs const express = require ( ' express ' ) const Router = express . Router // mjs import express , { Router } from ' express '
 //cjs const clientRouter = require ( ' express ' ). Router // mjs import { Router as clientRouter } from ' express '

出口

// cjs module . exports = express // mjs export default express
 // cjs module . exports = { router : {...} , utils : {...} } // mjs export { router : {...}, utils : {...} }

一些可能派上用场的更多导出模式

// mjs export default function hello () {...} export const bye = " bye "


原文:

To all the node developer you need to stop using require() in your new project. The node has already support for modules and this tutorial will tell you difference between them and what to use instead of require() and also will deep down into module a bit more.

What are you using

const express = require('express') // common js

What you should use

import express from 'express' // es module

Did you see ?? how much the better code look in second one. The first one is commonjs syntax which is present in node from its origin for importing libraries , the second one was first introduced in browser and then it came to node.

It makes code so much readable , modern and non - verbose.

How to use it ?

Its easy.

  1. Initialise new node project.
  2. Go to your package.json.

  3. Add following to it.

    "type" : "module" ,

  4. By default when you initialise your project its set to commonjs.

  5. That's it now start using modern javascript.

Common patterns

Instead of explaining it I am going to show you commonjs code implemented in module format, so that you can start it immediately , also comeback to this article in future when you are confuse how to do certain things in module format.

Importing 

// cjs
const express = require('express')
// mjs
import express from 'express'

// cjs
const express = require('express')
const Router = express.Router
// mjs
import express , { Router } from 'express'

//cjs
const clientRouter = require('express').Router
// mjs
import { Router as clientRouter } from 'express'

Exporting 

// cjs
module.exports = express
// mjs
export default express

// cjs
module.exports = { router : {...} , utils : {...}
}
// mjs
export { router : {...}, utils : {...}
}

Some more exporting pattern that may come handy 

// mjs
export default function hello() {...}
export const bye = "bye"

Items.Title: 公司如何才能让现有团队保持高效和快乐？ | How Can Companies Keep Their Existing Teams Productive & Happy?
Items.Description:
在日益火爆的就业市场中，一个行业正面临着日益严重的人才短缺问题，而且丝毫没有放缓的迹象； 技术。 过去几年，各行各业的企业都呼吁重新思考他们如何开展业务，这反过来又对负责帮助企业驾驭新的数字化转型的软件工程师产生了更大的需求。

伴随着人们所谓的“大辞职”的影响，对程序员的需求增加，导致美国和世界各地的技术人才出现巨大短缺。

根据 Gartner 进行的同一项研究，大多数企业认为人才短缺是阻碍其业务发展以满足宏观环境不断变化的需求的最大障碍。 虽然这种日益严重的技术人才短缺令人极为担忧，并因全球大流行而加剧，但早在 Covid-19 永远改变我们的工作生活之前，这种担忧就已经表达了。

早在 2010 年，超过 50% 的受访 CEO 表达了他们对缺乏数字人才的担忧。 到 2019 年，这一数字已增长到 79%。 因此，可以公平地说，大流行确实使对编码器的需求大大增加。

那么，在当今令人难以置信的有利就业市场中，软件工程师对气候有何反应？ 根据 TalentLMS 和 Workable 在 2022 年进行的一项调查，美国 72% 的科技员工正在强烈考虑在未来 12 个月内离职。 当谈到影响他们决策的因素时，40% 的人表示，有限的职业发展机会发挥了巨大的作用。 表达的其他原因包括不灵活的工作时间、缺乏远程工作选择、感觉被低估以及有毒的工作环境。 惊人的 85% 表示，他们认为他们的公司更注重吸引新人才，而不是投资现有员工。 也许不足为奇的是，58% 的人表示他们正遭受倦怠之苦。

因此，商业领袖需要将他们的技术人才放在首位，这已经不是什么秘密了，但在一个随着时间的推移获得此类人才变得越来越难的市场中，公司优先为现有团队配备资源和工具，他们需要在自己的角色中取得成功和快乐。

任何编码员都会告诉你，与他们的工作相关的重复性任务，例如代码审查和采购代码片段，不仅会影响他们的生产力，还会影响他们对性能的整体满意度。 根据 Openrefactory 进行的一项研究，开发人员 75% 的时间都花在了调试上——这大约是一年 1,500 小时。 更令人担忧的是，一项对软件开发人员生产力水平的研究发现，大多数人实际上花费不到一半的工作日来编写代码。

正确的工具可以改变世界。 我们的Coding Assistant 工具被世界各地数以万计的开发人员使用，旨在减少花费无数小时来采购代码片段的需求。

使用已由其他开发人员编写的代码片段是常见的做法。 这些可重用和适应性强的代码片段加速了项目并减少了编写已经存在的代码所浪费的时间。 编码员不能没有代码片段，但我们的编码助手工具具有智能代码片段，可以进一步简化您的工作流程。

使 Codiga 的智能代码片段更上一层楼的原因在于，用户可以访问不断增长的智能代码片段库，这些库已由使用我们平台的数千名其他开发人员添加和审查。 更好的是，这些智能代码片段可以在Codiga Hub上受到青睐（稍后会详细介绍），然后只需几个简短的命令即可直接填充到您选择的 IDE 上。

编码助手功能中的智能代码片段可以在您编写代码的任何地方工作，并且可以使用我们的 VS Code、IntelliJ 和 Chrome 插件轻松添加到他们的工作环境中。 这些插件支持所有语言，并且 Coding Assistant 还适用于基于云的开发平台，如 GitHub 代码空间、Replit 或 Gitpod。

使用 Coding Assistant 时，用户可以在 Coding Assistant Hub 上订阅我们网络中的任何公共智能代码片段。 这个有用的工具允许您根据与代码片段相关的语言、名称或关键字和标签来查找代码片段。 一旦您选择了智能代码片段，您就可以在您选择的环境中填充它，只需输入与智能代码片段相关的名称或关键字，然后继续使用可构建、值得信赖的代码片段。

除了减少在线搜索正确编码模式的时间外，编码助手还充当您自己的个人代码片段管理器，允许您添加自己的智能代码片段，您可以公开、私下或与您选择的团队共享。 智能代码片段可以在我们的 Web 界面上创建，也可以直接在您的 IDE 中创建。 要创建可共享的（请记住，您也可以将其保密！）智能代码片段，只需直接在他们的 IDE 中突出显示它，右键单击，然后选择创建智能代码片段。 从那里，这段代码将直接填充到我们的添加智能代码片段部分，您将被引导添加名称、语言、简短描述，最重要的是，将触发智能代码片段出现的关键字在编码助手中。 保存后，智能代码片段将立即可供使用。 添加和共享代码片段只需几分钟，从而为团队和个人编码人员节省大量时间，因为团队中的每个人都可以很容易地实时共享他们的工作，如果您独自工作，一个简单的命令现在将为您完成大量的手动工作。

包起来

原文:

In an increasingly hot job market, one industry is facing a growing shortage of talent that shows no signs of slowing down; Tech. The last few years have called for businesses in all sectors to rethink how they do business, and in turn, this has created an even larger demand for software engineers who are tasked with helping companies navigate the new, digital transformation.

A report from Gartner found that 58% of IT leaders reported either an increase or a plan to increase emerging technology investments in 2021, compared with 29% in 2020.
This increased demand for coders married with the fallout of The Great Resignation, as it has come to be called, has caused a massive deficit or tech talent in the United States and around the world.

According to the same study conducted by Gartner, the majority of businesses believe a shortage of talent to be the biggest obstacle standing in the way of growing their business to meet the ever-changing needs of the macro environment. While this growing shortage of tech talent is extremely concerning and exacerbated by the global pandemic, the concern has been expressed long before the Covid-19 changed our working lives forever.

As far back as 2010, more than 50% of CEOs polled expressed their concern over the lack of digital talent. By 2019, that number had grown to 79%. So, it is fair to say the pandemic did make the demand for coders a lot greater.

So, in today's incredibly favorable job market, how are software engineers reacting to the climate? According to a survey from TalentLMS and Workable conducted in 2022, 72% of tech employees in the United States are strongly considering leaving their current jobs in the next 12 months. When it comes to the factors influencing their decisions, 40% say that limited opportunities for career growth play a huge role. Other reasons expressed included non-flexible work hours, a lack of remote working options, feeling under-appreciated, and a toxic work environment. A staggering 85% said they felt their company focused more on attracting new talent than investing in existing staff. Unsurprisingly perhaps, 58% said they were suffering from burnout.

So, it’s no secret that business leaders need to have their tech talent top of mind, but in a market where acquiring such talent is becoming harder and harder as the days go in, it is absolutely vital that companies prioritize equipping their current team with the resources and tools they need to be successful and happy in their role.

Any coder will tell you that the repetitive tasks associated with their job, such as code reviews and sourcing code snippets, not only affect their productivity but their overall happiness with the performance. According to a study conducted by Openrefactory, 75% of a developer’s time is spent on debugging– that's roughly 1,500 hours a year. What's more concerning is that a study into the productivity levels of software developers found that the majority spend less than half of their workday actually writing code.

The right tool can make the world of difference. Codiga was designed by coders, for coders, and our platform houses a number of tools that have all been created to help developers cut their working time in half.

Our Coding Assistant tool, used by tens of thousands of developers from all over the world, was developed to mitigate the need for countless hours spent sourcing code snippets.

Using code snippets already coded by another developer is common practice. These reusable and adaptable pieces of code speed up projects and reduce wasted time spent writing code that already exists. Coders can't live without code snippets, but our Coding Assistant tool features Smart Code Snippets that will streamline your workflow even further.

What makes Codiga’s Smart Code Snippets next-level is the fact that users gain access to an ever-growing library of Smart Code Snippets that have been added and vetted by thousands of other developers who use our platform. Even better, these Smart Code Snippets can be favored on the Codiga Hub(more on this later) and will then populate directly on your chosen IDE with just a few short commands.

Smart Code Snippets, found within the Coding Assistant feature, work anywhere you write code and can be easily added to their working environment using our VS Code, IntelliJ, and Chrome plugins. These plugins support all languages, and Coding Assistant also works with cloud-based development platforms like GitHub codespaces, Replit or Gitpod.

When using Coding Assistant, a user can subscribe to any public Smart Code Snippet in our network on the Coding Assistant Hub. This helpful tool allows you to find code snippets based on language, name, or keyword and tag associated with the piece of code. Once you have favored a Smart Code Snippet, you can then populate it in your chosen environment by simply typing in the name or keyword associated with the Smart Code Snippet and then continue your work with a buildable, trustworthy piece of code.

In addition to cutting time searching for the right coding patterns online, Coding Assistant acts like your own personal Code Snippet Manager and allows you to add your own Smart Code Snippets that you can share publicly, privately, or with your selected team. Smart Code Snippets can be created on our web interface, or directly in your IDE. To create a shareable(remember you can keep it private, too!) Smart Code Snippet, simply highlight it directly in their IDE, right-click, and select create Smart Code Snippet. From there, the piece of code will populate directly in our add Smart Code Snippet section, and you’ll be directed to add a name, language, a brief description, and most importantly, the keywords that will trigger the Smart Code Snippet to appear in Coding Assistant. Once saved, the Smart Code Snippet will be available instantly for use. It takes a matter of minutes to add and share a code snippet, and results in a lot of time saved for both teams and individual coders, because it is very easy for everyone on your team to share their work with one another in real-time, and if you work alone, a simple command will now do a lot of the manual work for you moving forward.

Wrapping up

The tech talent shortage shows no signs of slowing down, but whether you’re an individual coder, or leading a team of developers during these uncertain times, know that the right tools can do wonders for productivity and job satisfaction.

If you’re interested in learning more about Codiga’s Smart Code Snippets, visit our site today and see how our tools can help you cut the unnecessary tasks from your workday.

Want to write for our blog? Reach out to contact@codiga.io with your pitch!

Items.Title: 在 Java 中将交通灯图标添加到 Excel | Add Traffic Lights Icons to Excel in Java
Items.Description:
在本文中，您将学习如何使用免费库——Free Spire.XLS for Java，以编程方式在 Excel 中添加交通灯图标。

导入Jar依赖（二法）

示例代码

import com.spire.xls.* ; import com.spire.xls.core.IConditionalFormat ; import com.spire.xls.core.spreadsheet.collections.XlsConditionalFormats ; import java.awt.* ; public class setTrafficLightsIcons { public static void main ( String [] args ) { //Create a workbook Workbook workbook = new Workbook (); //Add a worksheet Worksheet sheet = workbook . getWorksheets (). get ( 0 ); //Add data to cells sheet . getCellRange ( "A1" ). setText ( "Project Progress" ); sheet . getCellRange ( "A2" ). setNumberValue ( 0.95 ); sheet . getCellRange ( "A3" ). setNumberValue ( 0.5 ); sheet . getCellRange ( "A4" ). setNumberValue ( 0.1 ); sheet . getCellRange ( "A5" ). setNumberValue ( 0.9 ); sheet . getCellRange ( "A6" ). setNumberValue ( 0.7 ); sheet . getCellRange ( "A7" ). setNumberValue ( 0.6 ); //set number format for the cell range sheet . getCellRange ( "A2:A7" ). setNumberFormat ( "0%" ); //Set row height and column width for the cell range sheet . getAllocatedRange (). setRowHeight ( 20 ); sheet . getAllocatedRange (). setColumnWidth ( 25 ); //Add a conditional formatting XlsConditionalFormats conditional = sheet . getConditionalFormats (). add (); conditional . addRange ( sheet . getAllocatedRange ()); IConditionalFormat format1 = conditional . addCondition (); //Set the conditional format type, formula, comparison operator type and color format1 . setFormatType ( ConditionalFormatType . CellValue ); format1 . setFirstFormula ( "300" ); format1 . setOperator ( ComparisonOperatorType . Less ); format1 . setFontColor ( Color . black ); format1 . setBackColor ( Color . lightGray ); //Add a conditional formatting of traffic lights icon to the cell range conditional . addRange ( sheet . getAllocatedRange ()); IConditionalFormat format = conditional . addCondition (); format . setFormatType ( ConditionalFormatType . IconSet ); format . getIconSet (). setIconSetType ( IconSetType . ThreeTrafficLights1 ); //Save to file workbook . saveToFile ( "output/setTrafficLightsIcons.xlsx" , ExcelVersion . Version2013 ); } }



原文:

The traffic light icon in Excel is a conditional formatting mainly used to manage the progress and status of a project. In this article, you will learn how to programmatically add the traffic lights icons in Excel by using a free library--Free Spire.XLS for Java.

Import Jar Dependency (2 Method)

1# Download the free library and unzip it, then add the Spire.Xls.jar file to your project as dependency.
2# Directly add the jar dependency to maven project by adding the following configurations to the pom.xml.

<repositories> <repository> <id>com.e-iceblue</id> <name>e-iceblue</name> <url>http://repo.e iceblue.com/nexus/content/groups/public/</url> </repository>
</repositories>
<dependencies> <dependency> <groupId>e-iceblue</groupId> <artifactId>spire.xls.free</artifactId> <version>5.1.0</version> </dependency>
</dependencies>

Sample Code 

import com.spire.xls.*;
import com.spire.xls.core.IConditionalFormat;
import com.spire.xls.core.spreadsheet.collections.XlsConditionalFormats; import java.awt.*; public class setTrafficLightsIcons { public static void main(String[] args) { //Create a workbook Workbook workbook = new Workbook(); //Add a worksheet Worksheet sheet = workbook.getWorksheets().get(0); //Add data to cells sheet.getCellRange("A1").setText("Project Progress"); sheet.getCellRange("A2").setNumberValue(0.95); sheet.getCellRange("A3").setNumberValue(0.5); sheet.getCellRange("A4").setNumberValue(0.1); sheet.getCellRange("A5").setNumberValue(0.9); sheet.getCellRange("A6").setNumberValue(0.7); sheet.getCellRange("A7").setNumberValue(0.6); //set number format for the cell range sheet.getCellRange("A2:A7").setNumberFormat("0%"); //Set row height and column width for the cell range sheet.getAllocatedRange().setRowHeight(20); sheet.getAllocatedRange().setColumnWidth(25); //Add a conditional formatting XlsConditionalFormats conditional = sheet.getConditionalFormats().add(); conditional.addRange(sheet.getAllocatedRange()); IConditionalFormat format1 = conditional.addCondition(); //Set the conditional format type, formula, comparison operator type and color format1.setFormatType(ConditionalFormatType.CellValue); format1.setFirstFormula("300"); format1.setOperator(ComparisonOperatorType.Less); format1.setFontColor(Color.black); format1.setBackColor(Color.lightGray); //Add a conditional formatting of traffic lights icon to the cell range conditional.addRange(sheet.getAllocatedRange()); IConditionalFormat format = conditional.addCondition(); format.setFormatType(ConditionalFormatType.IconSet); format.getIconSet().setIconSetType(IconSetType.ThreeTrafficLights1); //Save to file workbook.saveToFile( "output/setTrafficLightsIcons.xlsx", ExcelVersion.Version2013); }
}

Items.Title: Express 不保存 cookie | Express doesn't save cookie
Items.Description:
虽然响应标头有“Set-Cookie: ...”，但我在浏览器的开发者工具中的 Application -> Cookie 中没有看到任何内容。 所以我也在后端（express）和前端（React 和 axios）修复了一些东西。

第 1 步：在后端更新配置凭据

目前，我正在使用 cors() 包。 所以我更新了 cors() 的凭据

const app = express (); // Setting credentials app . use ( cors ({ origin : ' http://localhost:3000 ' , // it depends on your site credentials : true , })); // Setting cookies res . cookie ( ' cookie-for-me ' , ' hello-world ' );

第 2 步：在后端更新配置凭据

这一步之后就差不多完成了。

 axios . defaults . withCredentials = true

最终：运行并检查您的 cookie

您可以在浏览器的开发者工具控制台输入document.cookie来查看结果。

如果您对保存 cookie 的方式有任何建议，请随时在帖子下方发表评论：)



原文:

Although the response header has 'Set-Cookie: ...' but I didn't see anything at Application -> Cookie in Developer tool of the browser. So I fixed something at the backend (express) and frontend (React and axios) too.

Step 1: Update configuration credentials at Backend side

Currently, I was use the cors() package. So I update credentials for cors()

const app = express(); // Setting credentials
app.use(cors({ origin: 'http://localhost:3000', // it depends on your site credentials: true,
})); // Setting cookies
res.cookie('cookie-for-me', 'hello-world');

Step 2: Update configuration credentials at Backend side

It almost done after this step.

axios.defaults.withCredentials = true

Final: Run and checking your cookie

You can check by enter document.cookie at the console of the developer tool of a browser to see the result.

If you have any suggestion about the way saving the cookie, please feel free comment as the following the post :)
Items.Author: maole

Items.Title: 帮助您获得工作的面试技巧 | Interview Tips that will Help You Get the Job
Items.Description:
伊桑·赫斯特

发表于通过工作面试不仅仅是在谷歌上搜索一系列面试问题和答案。 从研究“关于公司”到准备“常见的面试问题”，你需要做好准备，把自己最好的一面展现出来。

除了知识和经验外，你还需要有效的沟通和坚定的果断等软技能，才能在求职面试中留下良好的第一印象。 以下是一系列面试技巧，可帮助您解决开放式、情境和行为面试问题。

所有工作角色的面试技巧

遵循这些面试技巧，展示你的技能、能力和才能，以完美地完成你的工作面试。 证明您非常适合特定工作，并说服他们通过录取通知书将您带入公司。

准备是成功的关键从常见的面试问题开始，例如“告诉我你自己”和“提及你的优势”。 您也可以在面试前向招聘人员询问面试形式，并按照说明进行准备。 如果你在没有做好面试准备的情况下参加面试，你会听起来很乱，很困惑。

提前做好面试准备可以防止你被诸如“陈述你最大的错误”和“为什么离开以前的工作”之类的问题所困扰。 请记住，您的第一印象很重要！
招聘专家声称，面试官会在面试的最初几分钟内做出决定。 最好的面试技巧是自信地进入，以坚定的心态表达想法，并令人信服地回答面试问题。

明确你的技能和能力
最重要的面试技巧是你必须提及你的技能，并解释你在实现目标的同时如何利用这些技能。 以面试官认为合理的方式传达你的能力。 陈述您探索的工作经验、您面临的实际现实以及您采取的方法。 这将有助于面试官详细了解您的技能，并增加您被录用的机会。

设身处地为面试官着想
如果您这样做，您将能够以更好的方式调整您的答案。

他们的主要要求是什么？ 他们正在寻找什么能力？ 哪些技能会让你成为这份工作的合适人选？ 您将如何管理前所未有的工作场所情况？
在您到达公司进行面试之前，请准备好这些面试问题和答案。 这也将帮助您满足他们的员工需求，并让他们确信您正是他们正在寻找的人。 遵循此面试技巧将增加您的就业机会。

自信地展示自己
当您进入面试室或放大会议时，请确保您给人留下强烈而自信的印象。 一些常见的面试技巧是 -在交流时进行直接的眼神交流，在你进入时提供坚定的握手，用自信的声音说话，并保持积极的姿势。 如果你自信地展示自己，你可以抓住很多机会。

参考常见的面试问题
几乎每个面试指南都包含常见的面试问题和答案。 这将防止在面试期间回答时颤抖。

为成功着装
你的着装方式肯定会影响招聘人员的招聘决定。 在面试当天，穿着专业，看起来很整洁。 考虑到你申请的公司文化和工作概况，着装总是一个好主意。 如果您通过任何参考与公司联系，请了解公司的着装要求并相应地计划您的着装。

培养有效的沟通技巧有效的沟通技巧在职业生活的几乎每个方面都至关重要。 几乎没有任何工作不需要强大的沟通技巧。 雇主认为，人际交往能力得到提高的候选人可以极大地促进团队建设并取得富有成效的成果。

作为面试者，多与面试官交流，提出后续问题，并在回答面试问题时展示你的社交技巧。

挖掘关于公司的每一分钟细节。 阅读他们最近的新闻稿，了解他们最新的产品开发或服务，并查看他们的博客、社交媒体账户。

通过更好地了解公司，您将能够以可观的方式公平地回答问题。 您可以利用它来验证您对诸如“为什么是这家公司”之类的问题的回答。 阅读职位描述
这让您了解招聘人员可能会问的问题。 专注于提到的技能，回忆你过去的工作经历，并尝试将它们与面试官的期望相匹配。 您可以根据职位描述中指定的要求定制您的答案。

聪明地回答问题你应该尽早开始准备你的面试。 通过面试技巧工作不仅可以帮助您获得工作，还可以为您提供一种聪明的思维方式，这种思维方式将通过解决问题的方法获得经验。

例如，如果您需要时间来填补空白并回答棘手的问题，请准备好要使用的短语。 您可以在回答之前深思熟虑地重复问题，也可以回答“有趣的问题！ 我宁愿…… ”

列出面试官的问题
以证明您对公司的兴趣的方式提出问题。 你可能会被问到，'你想问什么吗？ 出现在积极的音符上
在面试中，积极回答每一个问题。 无论是开放式面试问题还是行为面试问题，都不要在回答时纠缠于消极方面。 当面试官询问您的工作经历时，请提及积极的经历。 面试官一直在寻找无论工作场所情况如何都具有积极态度的员工。

阿扬·班纳吉—— 阿扬·班纳吉—— Jul 7 '20 20 年 7 月 7 日



原文:

Ethan Hest

Posted on May 11

Getting through a job interview takes a lot more than googling a list of interview questions and answers. From researching ‘about the company’ to preparing for ‘common interview questions,’ you need to prepare yourself to put your best foot forward.

Apart from knowledge and experience, you’ll require soft skills like effective communication and firm decisiveness to leave a good first impression during the job interview. Here’s a list of interview tips that will help you get through open-ended, situational and behavioral interview questions.

Interview Tips for All Job Roles

Follow these interview tips and showcase your skills, abilities, and talent to ace your job interview perfectly. Demonstrate that you’re the perfect fit for a particular job profile and convince them to bring you on board with an offer letter.

Preparation is a key to success Begin with the commonly asked interview questions such as ‘tell me about yourself' and ‘mention your strengths.’ You can also ask a recruiter about the interview format ahead of the interview and prepare as stated. If you appear for an interview without doing interview preparation well, you’ll sound garbled and confused.

Prepping for the job interview well in advance will prevent you from getting thrown off by questions such as ‘state your biggest mistake’ and ‘why you left your previous job.’

Remember, your first impression matters!
Recruitment experts claim that interviewers make up their minds within the first few minutes of interview. The best interview tip is to enter with confidence, express ideas with a firm mind, and answer interview questions convincingly.

Make your skills and abilities clear
The most appreciable interview tip is you must mention your skills and explain how you utilized those skills while accomplishing goals. Communicate your abilities in a way that seems sound to your interviewer. State work experiences you explored, practical realities you faced and approaches you took. This will help interviewer gain detailed knowledge about your skillset and increase your chances of getting hired.

Put yourself in the shoes of interviewer
If you do so, you’ll be able to tailor your answers in a better way.

What are their key requirements? What are the capabilities they are looking for? What skills will make you a suitable candidate for the job? How will you manage unprecedented workplace situations?
Prepare these interview questions and answers before you reach the company for an interview. This will also help you meet their employee needs and lend them reassurance that you are exactly what they’re looking for. Following this interview tip will increase your employment possibility.

Present yourself with confidence
When you enter the interview room or zoom meeting, ensure you make a strong and confident impression. Some common interview tips are - make direct eye contact while communicating, offer a firm handshake as you enter, speak with a confident voice, and have a positive posture. If you present yourself with confidence, you can pull off plenty of opportunities.

Refer commonly asked interview questions
Almost every interview guide consists of commonly asked ‘interview questions and answers.’ Refer to your job profile and prepare the questions that are most likely to encounter. This will prevent trembling while answering during the interview.

Dress for success
The way you dress definitely affects the recruiter’s hiring decision. On the day of the interview look well-groomed by dressing professionally. It’s always a good idea to dress by considering the company culture and job profile you’re applying for. If you’ve approached the company by any reference, know the company dress code and plan your outfit accordingly.

Develop effective communication skills Effective communication skills are vital in almost every aspect of professional life. There hardly exists any job that doesn’t require strong communication skills. Employers believe that candidates with improved interpersonal skills highly promote team building and deliver productive results.

As an interviewee, communicate more with your interviewer, ask follow back questions, and bear out your social skills while answering interview questions.

*Know your company * While doing interview preparation, learn everything about the company – its products, services, employee benefits, success story, and more. Tap on every minute detail you can about the company. Read their recent press releases, know about their latest product development or service offering, and check their blogs, social media accounts.

By knowing the company better, you’ll be able to answer the questions fairly in an appreciable way. You can utilize this to validate your answer to questions like ‘why this company?’

Read the job description
This gives you an idea about the questions a recruiter can possibly ask. Focus on the skills mentioned, recall your past work experiences, and try to match them with the interviewer’s expectations. You can tailor your answers according to requirements specified in the job description.

Answer questions smartly You should start preparing for your interview as early as possible. Working through interview tips will not only help you get the job but also lend you a smart mindset that’ll get experienced with a problem-solving approach.

For instance, get ready with the phrases to be used if you need time to fill the gap while you and answer tricky questions. You can either repeat question thoughtfully prior to answering or answer like – “Interesting question! I would prefer…”

List questions for the interviewer
Ask questions in a way that justifies your interests in the company. You’ll likely be asked, ‘do you want to ask anything?’ If you respond ‘no’ to this, your interviewer might perceive that you’re uninterested in the job.

Appear on a positive note
During an interview, answer every question positively. Whether it be open-ended or behavioral interview questions, don’t dwell on negative aspects while answering. When the interviewer asks about your work experiences, mention the positive ones. Interviewers are constantly looking for employees who have a positive approach irrespective of workplace situations.

Items.Title: 算命 | Fortune telling
Items.Description:
发表于在我所处的世界里，描述类型签名的代码比告诉程序做什么的代码更长。 我不想把这种情况带回来。

我希望 AI 代码完成能够取代静态类型。 Ruby Type Signature (RBS) and similar things must be inferred automatically from a database or other data sources. Ruby 类型签名 (RBS) 和类似的东西必须从数据库或其他数据源自动推断。

Intesar Shannan Mohammed - Intesar Shannan Mohammed - May 10 5月10日



原文:

Vee Satayamas

Posted on May 11

I was in a world where a code describing type signature was longer than a code telling what a program does. I don't want to bring that situation back.

I expect AI code completion to replace static typing. Ruby Type Signature (RBS) and similar things must be inferred automatically from a database or other data sources.

Intesar Shannan Mohammed - May 10
